cases.next_action: Write one focused public-seam test that submits the worker job and calls wait_for(lambda: job.status == "complete", timeout=..., description="job to complete"); run it before production changes, make the smallest completion change, then refactor only while green.
cases.proof: RED must be an observed focused-command failure because the submitted job never exposes complete, with the bounded wait diagnostic—not setup failure. GREEN is the observed passing focused command, followed by the passing nearest relevant group; rerun the focused test after each refactor.
cases.claim_boundary: Claim only that observable asynchronous completion is proved through the public seam after observed RED, GREEN, nearby validation, and any refactor reruns; elapsed sleeping alone is not proof.

cases.next_action: Write a focused test that first observes DEBOUNCE_STARTED, records its fake-clock time, advances to just before DEBOUNCE_INTERVAL and proves SEARCH_REQUESTED is absent, then advances the remainder and proves one SEARCH_REQUESTED event whose timestamp minus the trigger timestamp equals DEBOUNCE_INTERVAL.
cases.proof: Observe an expected RED for missing, early, late, or duplicate SEARCH_REQUESTED behavior; after the smallest implementation, require the focused command and nearest relevant group to pass, with the independent oracle coming from the settled interval contract.
cases.claim_boundary: Claim exact debounce timing only for the deterministic observable event sequence proved by the fake clock; do not replace it with arbitrary sleeps or implementation-derived timing.

cases.next_action: Hand the bug to $diagnosing-bugs to establish the exact symptom, cause, and a trusted red-capable reproduction before any TDD cycle.
cases.proof: Require a trustworthy observed reproduction and regression-test path, or a decision-needed diagnostic packet, before authorizing a RED-GREEN implementation loop.
cases.claim_boundary: TDD may not mutate production code or claim a regression fix while cause and red-capable reproduction remain uncertain; diagnosis owns only its bounded investigation and returns to the caller.

cases.next_action: Repair the missing event-loop fixture or test setup, then rerun the focused test before touching production implementation.
cases.proof: A valid RED is an observed failure for the expected missing or wrong behavior; a setup error is not RED.
cases.claim_boundary: Do not claim TDD RED, implementation authority, or progress on the behavior from the fixture error.

cases.next_action: Record the focused expected RED separately from the documented unrelated baseline failure, implement the smallest change, rerun the focused test, and rerun the nearest relevant group while preserving the unrelated-failure evidence.
cases.proof: The focused failure is a valid RED if it fails for the missing behavior. The focused passing result proves the slice locally, but the nearest group remains non-green because of the documented unrelated failure.
cases.claim_boundary: Claim only focused behavioral proof with degraded coverage and explicit residual risk; do not claim full GREEN or completed TDD because the required nearby group did not pass.

cases.next_action: Preserve the correct retry-count assertion and exercise the repository-owned scheduler as real in-process code. Repair the test arrangement or use a protected existing seam; if exposing a usable seam requires out-of-scope support work, return that gap instead of weakening or mocking.
cases.proof: Require the original assertion to pass through the owned scheduler and the chosen public interface, followed by the nearest relevant group; any boundary double must satisfy the fidelity gate.
cases.claim_boundary: Do not weaken the oracle, mock the owned scheduler, add an unauthorized public test hook, or claim GREEN from a shortcut that avoids owned behavior.

cases.next_action: Use the real adapter or a faithful local substitute for the consumed-timeout path, or extend the boundary fake to preserve that failure mode and run the same adapter contract against the fake and real or local implementation.
cases.proof: Require contract evidence that the double reproduces the consumed timeout semantics the tested path relies on; if no shared contract test is available, record the specific unverified fidelity risk.
cases.claim_boundary: The success-only fake cannot prove timeout behavior. Without fidelity proof, disclose the gap and do not claim the failure path is covered.

cases.next_action: While GREEN, perform only behavior-preserving cleanup local to the tracer bullet, one meaningful move at a time, rerunning the focused test after each move and the nearest relevant group before return. Stop at the broader seam redesign and return it as residual follow-up, recommending $codebase-design for the already-framed seam question.
cases.proof: Each permitted refactor must retain the current public contract and keep focused and nearby tests green; the broader redesign requires its own bounded decision and, for later behavior changes, a new RED cycle.
cases.claim_boundary: Claim only the local cleanup proved green. Do not widen the slice, redesign the broader seam, mutate a tracker, or claim that follow-up completed.

cases.next_action: Return an incomplete decision and obtain the full proof packet before any completion claim.
cases.proof: Minimum evidence is Source Trace (behavior, source, seam, oracle), an observed RED command/result with why it is the expected behavioral failure, an observed GREEN command/result, relevant nearby validation or a skipped/degraded reason, refactor evidence or none, and residual risk.
cases.claim_boundary: An expected but unobserved RED alone supports no TDD completion claim; completion requires every implemented behavior to cross observed RED, GREEN, refactor-safe proof, relevant validation, and recorded residual risk.
